items.title: This World Prematurity Day, Pampers is enabling essential moments of bonding, as 92% of parents of premature babies said that physical touch made them feel more connected to their little ones[1]
items.description: Touch is crucial to all babies' development, but with skin twice as thin as that of a full-term baby, premature babies' skin can often be very fragile[2]. Despite this vulnerability, skin-to-skin contact is crucial for their development, as it significantly boosts oxytocin[3] levels, an important hormone for familial bonding.

items.title: Radius Insights Appoints Former P&G Executive Michael Lancor to its Board of Directors
items.description: Radius Insights, a leading global market research and insights firm, has appointed Michael Lancor, former Vice President of Analytics and Insights at Procter & Gamble, to its Board of Directors.
